sentiment: The world hides in plain sight — colors our screens refuse to show, gardens in pixels smaller than a pin.

stories.title: I Stored a Website in a Favicon
stories.summary: A playful technical feat — compressing an entire website into the 16x16 pixel canvas of a favicon, proving that even the tiniest vessel can hold a world.

stories.title: Where to Find the Colors Your Screen Can't Show You
stories.summary: An exploration of the colors that exist beyond the gamut of modern displays — ultraviolet, infrared, and the shimmering in-between — and where in nature you can still glimpse them.

stories.title: Can you see three trees?
stories.summary: A meditation on attention and perception — how the most present things in our environment become invisible through familiarity, and what it takes to see them again.
